name: Entenmann's Apple Crumb Cake
recipeIngredient: 4 Granny Smith Apples, peeled and sliced to 1/2 inch pieces
1 package Active Dry Yeast, dissolved in 3/4 cup Warm Water (120 F)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large skillet.
2. Add apples to the melted butter and cook, stirring, for 8 minutes until tender.
3. Stir in sugar, lemon peel, cinnamon, mace, and currants; cook for an additional 15 minutes until thickened.
4. Cool completely.
5. In a large bowl, mix together 1 cup flour, 3/4 cup sugar, salt, and yeast dissolved in warm water.
6. In a small saucepan, combine butter with remaining 3/4 cup warm water; heat on low flame until 120 F.
7. Gradually add the hot butter mixture to the dry ingredients; mix for 2 minutes.
8. Beat in egg and lemon peel.
9. Mix in additional 1/4 cup flour.
10. Cover and let rest at room temperature for 20 minutes.
11. Grease two baking sheets with butter.
12. On a well-floured surface, roll dough to a rectangle measuring approximately 14" x 12".
13. Place the dough on one of the prepared sheets; spread filling lengthwise down the center of the dough.
14. Starting about 3/4 inch from the filling, cut diagonal strips (about 1 inch wide).
15. Fold opposite strips of dough across the filling at angles; fold ends over the filling.
16. Brush a large piece of waxed paper with vegetable oil and loosely cover one sheet.
17. Top with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours.
18. Preheat oven to 375 F (190 C).
19. Combine remaining ingredients for topping in a small bowl.
20. Sprinkle the mixture over the cooled loaves.
21. Bake for 30-25 minutes, or until lightly browned; remove from sheets and cool.
